title: Algolia Search Contract & Index Schema
purpose: Define the Algolia search index schema, client configuration, and TypeScript contracts needed by frontend search. Provides the interface boundary between search consumers (frontend) and search producers (backend sync).
architectural_constraints: Index schema is source of truth for searchable/filterable attributes
TypeScript types must be isomorphic (shared between frontend and backend)
Client configuration is environment-driven (app ID, search-only API key)
No runtime dependency on backend sync implementation

deliverables.description: Index configuration: searchable attributes, filters, ranking
TypeScript types for Hit, Facet, Filter, SearchParams
Frontend Algolia search client initialization (search-only key)

acceptance_criteria: TypeScript types compile in both frontend and backend surfaces
Frontend can import search contracts from @/shared/contracts/search
Index schema documents all searchable, filterable, and sortable attributes
Client config supports environment-based API key rotation
No imports from backend search implementation

traceability.description: Implemented Algolia search contract suite: index schema, result types, filter/facet/sort contracts, config shape, adapter interfaces

failure_modes.scenario: Schema drift between frontend and backend
failure_modes.mitigation: Single shared contract file; both surfaces import from same source
